description: Keep request validation, model binding, cancellation, and error shape aligned with existing API contracts.
body: Use when changing controllers, minimal APIs, middleware, filters, or DTOs. Do not move validation into deep business logic if the route contract owns it. Inspect existing error conventions and cancellation token flow. Verify with route/unit/integration tests or a local HTTP smoke. Risk boundary: response shape and status codes are client contracts. Use when C# / .NET Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
